title: Networking Equipment Selector — Switch Knowledge Base Schema
description: Series-agnostic shape + authoring contract for a switch knowledge base in the Networking Equipment Selector project. This schema enforces SHAPE (entries are well-formed); the switching-axes.json registry enforces MEANING (which axes exist, their legal enum values); validate2.py enforces CROSS-REFERENCE INTEGRITY (group references resolve, valid_primary is a subset of the referenced PSU group, poe_budget_watts equals the matrix max, enum values are registered). All three must pass. This file is also written to be read as an instruction-set: see $authoring for the procedure an agent follows to add a model.

$authoring._comment: NON-VALIDATING. Procedure + rules for an agent (e.g. Claude Code) populating new switch models. Read this before touching data.
$authoring.prime_directive: Push everything into DATA; keep the solver small and dumb. Never encode a compatibility quirk as logic. If a relationship between specs exists (speed subsumption, PSU-pair budgets, tier locks), express it as data in this KB, not as code elsewhere.
sourcing_discipline: All facts come from authoritative Cisco documentation ONLY — never from memory or inference.
Derive the fetch targets from the KB header 'sources' block (datasheet URL + ordering guide URL). Do not construct independent web searches.
Fetch the datasheet AND the ordering guide before proposing any values for a model.
Record how each look-through / resolved axis value was derived in the model's axis_value_provenance block, citing the datasheet table or ordering-guide section.
$authoring.working_copy_discipline: Project files under /mnt/project are READ-ONLY. Copy to a working location first; all edits happen on the working copy. When catalog/group state becomes uncertain mid-session, rebuild deterministically from an explicit list rather than patching incrementally.

cheap_do_freely: Add a new catalog SKU (network module, PSU, cable, license).
Add a new group, even one whose membership duplicates an existing group (deliberate redundancy is preferred when it preserves future room to diverge).
structural_STOP_and_ask: Adding, renaming, or re-typing an AXIS in switching-axes.json (touches UI + MCP params + maybe solver). Bump registry_version only after human sign-off.
Adding a new legal enum value to an existing axis.
Changing group topology conventions (how groups are split: e.g. license split on port-class+tier, PSU groups per series).
Any change to this schema's required-field or conditional rules.

axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.rule: Sort every datasheet property three ways before deciding where it goes.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.axis: A filterable capability ('can this switch do X?'). Goes in switching-axes.json and is supplied as a value under model.axis_values. Example: poe_budget_watts, uplink_10g, stacking_capable.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.variable: The orderable option that REALIZES a capability, picked AFTER a switch survives the filter. Lives inside model.configurables (a group reference or an inline block). NOT an axis, no registry entry, no UI control. Example: the specific PSU pair, the fitted network module, the subscription term length.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.attribute: Displayed, never filtered, never picked. Lives loose under model.attributes. Example: switching_capacity_gbps, weight, MTBF.

capability_resolution.uplinks: Per-speed uplink counts are NEVER stored on the switch. They resolve against an uplink_capacity.per_speed_max block. On modular models (uplink_modular=true) the block lives on the referenced network-module group (look-through). On fixed-uplink models (uplink_modular=false) the switch carries an inline uplink_capacity block of the same shape. Because of this, axis_values MUST NOT contain any uplink_* key.
capability_resolution.poe_budget: poe_budget_watts is the MAX across the switch's configurables.power_supplies.poe_budget_matrix ({primary, secondary} -> watts). The PSU pair that delivers a requested wattage is read from the matrix at solve time.
capability_resolution.speed_subsumption: Declare real per-speed capability as DATA (e.g. a 25G fiber port that also does 10G/1G is counted under access_25g_fiber AND access_10g_fiber AND access_1g_fiber). Do not rely on the solver to infer 25G-implies-10G. Known asymmetry: uplink modules currently record native speed only; module-speed subsumption is a flagged future pass, not yet declared.

per_model_procedure: 1. Read the KB header 'sources'; fetch datasheet + ordering guide for the target model.
2. For every property the datasheet exposes, apply axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute. If it implies a NEW axis or NEW enum value -> STOP, surface for ruling.
3. Fill axis_values with the registry axes (omit optional axes that don't apply; e.g. PoE axes absent on no-PoE models, fiber-access axes absent on copper-only models). Never add uplink_* here.

4. Build configurables: reference existing groups where the set matches; create a new group if the set differs (cheap). Build the poe_budget_matrix from the datasheet PSU table; set poe_budget_watts = matrix max.
5. Fill axis_value_provenance for every resolved/look-through value, citing the source table.
7. Validate: this schema (shape) + validate2.py (cross-reference integrity). Both must pass before the entry is considered done.
conventions: License groups are split on BOTH port-class and tier (a 24-port DNA-E set differs from a 48-port DNA-E set). Tier is locked by the -E/-A hardware suffix; the subscription tier must match the network-stack tier.
Term length (3/5/7-yr) is a VARIABLE, not an axis — it lives in the license group's term_variable, never in the registry.

PSU groups represent the COMPLETE orderable family for a series, not just what one switch's PoE matrix needs. Prune a non-Platinum PSU only where a Platinum twin exists at the same wattage. Retain DC variants for future filtering.
The -M (Meraki) variant is a DISTINCT model entry, not a toggle. It carries meraki-classic / meraki-subscription regimes.
Series-specific groups (PSU, stacking cables) are kept as their own named groups even when membership currently matches another series, so they can diverge later.
total_port_count minus multigig_port_count yields the 1G-remainder copper ports; no extra axis is needed for that remainder.

models.description: Switch entries. Each is a flat, self-contained model: supplies axis values, references shared groups, carries only the genuinely switch-specific overlay. No inheritance between sibling variants (-E vs -A vs -M).

header.description: Provenance + versioning for the whole KB.

schema_version.$comment: semver of THIS schema the KB conforms to.

registry_version.$comment: semver of switching-axes.json the KB's axis_values conform to.

axes_file.$comment: Relative path to the authoritative axis registry (split out of the KB).

sources.description: Authoritative Cisco documents. An agent derives all fetch targets from here — it does not invent searches.

cisco_updated.$comment: Date Cisco last revised the doc, if shown.

last_verified.$comment: Date a human/agent last checked KB values against this doc.

catalog.description: Atomic SKU facts: one row per real orderable component, independent of which switch uses it.

provides_uplinks.description: Per-speed uplink count this single module provides. Keys are uplink_* axis names. These feed the group's aggregated uplink_capacity.per_speed_max.

compatible_family.$comment: Series this module fits. Legal values defined by the series axis in switching-axes.json.

rating.$comment: e.g. 'Platinum', or null for unrated/non-Platinum units retained for filtering.

type.$comment: Cable family, e.g. type-1 / type-3a. Not a registry axis.

regime.$comment: license_regime axis value; legal values in switching-axes.json.

port_class.$comment: null when the SKU carries no port-count (e.g. the C9300X -L- fiber DNA SKUs serve multiple port counts).

groups.description: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by name and inherits the whole set. The sharing unit is the SET, not the SKU. Update Cisco's family once -> every referencing switch inherits.

members.$comment: catalog network_module ids; validate2 checks they resolve.

none_option.$comment: CCW SKU used to remove the category when no module is required.

uplink_capacity.description: Resolved per-speed uplink capacity. The uplink_* axes are checked against per_speed_max; no per-speed value is stored on the switch. Lives on a network-module group (modular models) OR inline on a fixed-uplink model — identical shape, different location. The inline (fixed) form omits bay_count.

bay_count.$comment: Number of module bays. Present on group form. With bay_count 1 the speeds are mutually exclusive (one module fitted). Omitted on fixed-uplink inline form.

per_speed_max.description: Max provided by a single module/bay per speed.

power_supply_group.description: Complete orderable PSU family for a series (NOT just what one switch's PoE matrix needs). Which member is a valid PRIMARY and the PoE budget per pair are switch-specific and live on the model.

stack_kit.$comment: Fixed-uplink families stack via a kit (e.g. C9300L-STACK-KIT2). Absent on modular families that stack via cables alone.

license_group.description: A license SET keyed on regime + tier + port_class. A model links to the set, not a single license. Subscription is mandatory; tier is locked by the hardware suffix. Term length is a downstream variable.

applies_to.$comment: Optional human-readable scoping note (e.g. which models a port_class:null set serves).

perpetual_member.$comment: catalog license id of the embedded network-stack perpetual license. null for Meraki regimes (no perpetual SKU exists).

axis.$comment: Conventionally 'n/a' — term length is a VARIABLE, not a registry axis.

model.description: One switch SKU. Flat and self-contained.

axis_value_provenance.description: How each resolved/look-through axis value was derived, citing the source table. Free-form keys (often globbed, e.g. 'uplink_*', 'poe_*').

attributes.description: Displayed, never filtered, never picked. Free-form display facts (capacity, scale, dimensions, weight, MTBF, port_layout, fixed_uplinks string, default PSU, etc.).

included_by_default_non_selectable.description: Ships with the switch; not user-configurable, not filterable.

axis_values.description: Values this switch supplies on the registry axes. additionalProperties is FALSE: only declared axes may appear, which structurally forbids uplink_* keys here (uplinks resolve through capacity blocks, never stored on the switch). Optional axes are omitted when they do not apply.

poe_type.$comment: enum incl. 'none'; legal values in switching-axes.json.

poe_budget_watts.$comment: = max of configurables.power_supplies.poe_budget_matrix. Present only when poe_capable=true.

multigig_port_count.$comment: COPPER (RJ-45) Multigigabit access ports. 0 for non-mGig / fiber-only models.

access_1g_fiber.$comment: FIBER/SFP access ports doing 1G. Present only on fiber-access models. Overlaps with 10g/25g on multi-speed ports.

uplink_modular.$comment: true -> configurables.network_modules (group, look-through capacity). false -> configurables.uplinks (inline capacity).

stacking_technology.$comment: enum; required when stacking_capable=true. legal values in switching-axes.json.

license_regime.description: SET of regimes a model OFFERS (enum-set). Couples to switch selection as an ordinary hard constraint.

allOf.$comment: PoE present-ness: when poe_capable=true, require budget + port count and forbid poe_type 'none'.
No-PoE models: poe_type must be 'none' and PoE-only axes must be absent.
Stacking technology is required exactly when the switch stacks.

configurables.description: Thin references to shared groups + the switch-specific overlay only where facts vary per model. Exactly one of network_modules (modular) / uplinks (fixed) is present, matching axis_values.uplink_modular (cross-field consistency enforced by validate2).

ssd_accessory.description: Optional required-accessory variable (e.g. C9300LM SSD kit). Not an axis.

configurable_power_supplies.description: References a PSU group + the switch-specific overlay: which members are valid as PRIMARY, and the PoE budget per (primary, secondary) pair.

group.$comment: power_supply_groups id; validate2 checks valid_primary is a subset of its members.

poe_budget_matrix.description: (primary, secondary) -> watts. EMPTY for no-PoE models. poe_budget_watts axis = max poe_budget_watts across rows.
